seo.title: Building Data Extraction with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building data extraction pipelines with AutoGen enables multi-agent systems to parse unstructured documents into structured JSON schemas through collaborative LLM conversations. Guardrails are essential to prevent infinite loops, incorrect extractions, and schema violations that could lead to data loss or costly reprocessing. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs by validating schema compliance and field accuracy in real-time.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
The typical AutoGen data extraction architecture involves a Supervisor Agent that orchestrates specialized Extractor Agents. Each Extractor focuses on specific document sections or schema fields, debating and refining extractions collaboratively. A final Validator Agent checks completeness and type consistency before outputting structured JSON.

Documents are chunked and routed to agents via function calls, with conversation memory maintained across turns. Human-in-the-loop approval gates can be inserted for high-value extractions. The system scales by adding domain-specific agents for invoices, contracts, or medical records.

Vex integrates as a reply filter to catch failures early, validating Pydantic models against extraction schemas before agents proceed.

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents repeatedly challenge each other's extractions without converging on a final answer
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces max-turn limits and convergence criteria in reply filters

risks.name: Schema compliance failures
risks.description: Extracted JSON violates Pydantic models (missing required fields, wrong types)
risks.mitigation: Vex validates against extraction schema before reply acceptance

risks.name: Field accuracy drift
risks.description: Agents agree on plausible but factually incorrect extractions through groupthink
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-verifies key fields against source document embeddings

risks.name: Incomplete extractions
risks.description: Agents overlook optional fields or fail to extract all document entities
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness checks ensure all schema fields are populated

implementation.title: Define Extraction Schema with Pydantic
implementation.description: Create a Pydantic model defining the target structured output for document extraction.
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List

class Address(BaseModel):
    street: str = Field(..., description="Full street address")
    city: str
    zip_code: Optional[str] = None

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str
    date: str
    total_amount: float
    vendor: str
    customer_address: Address
    line_items: List[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)

    class Config:
        json_schema_extra = {"example": {...}}

implementation.title: Setup AutoGen Agents for Extraction
implementation.description: Configure supervisor and extractor agents with clear roles for collaborative extraction.
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, config_list_from_json

config_list = config_list_from_json("OAI_CONFIG_LIST")

supervisor = AssistantAgent(
    name="Supervisor",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
    system_message="You route document chunks to specialized extraction agents."
)

extractor = AssistantAgent(
    name="Extractor",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
    system_message="Extract invoice data into Pydantic Invoice model. Be precise."
)

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    name="User",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=5,
    code_execution_config=False
)

implementation.title: Initiate Multi-Agent Extraction
implementation.description: Start the conversation with document text and schema instructions.
implementation.code:
document_text = "Invoice #INV-1234 dated 2024-01-15... Total: $1,250.00..."

user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    supervisor,
    message=f"""
Extract this invoice using Invoice Pydantic schema:
\n{document_text}

Output ONLY valid JSON matching the schema."""
)

implementation.title: Parse and Validate Final Output
implementation.description: Convert agent response to Pydantic model and handle validation errors.
implementation.code:
import json

response = extractor.last_message()["content"]
try:
    extracted_invoice = Invoice.model_validate_json(response)
    print(f"✅ Valid extraction: {extracted_invoice.total_amount}")
except ValueError as e:
    print(f"❌ Schema violation: {e}")
    # Trigger human review or agent retry

vexIntegration.title: Install and Initialize Vex SDK
vexIntegration.description: Setup Vex client with your API key for AutoGen integration.
vexIntegration.code:
pip install autogen vex-sdk

import vex

vex_client = vex.Vex(api_key="vex_abc123")

# Register default verifiers for data extraction
vex_client.register_verifier(
    "extraction_schema",
    verifier="pydantic_validate",
    schema=Invoice.model_json_schema()
)

vex_client.register_verifier(
    "field_accuracy",
    verifier="semantic_match",
    threshold=0.9
)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Reply Function Filter
vexIntegration.description: Intercept agent replies and validate before conversation continues.

vexIntegration.title: Configure GroupChat with Vex Guardrails
vexIntegration.description: Setup GroupChat with turn limits and Vex monitoring for production use.
vexIntegration.code:
from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager

groupchat = GroupChat(
    agents=[supervisor, extractor, validator],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10,  # Prevent infinite loops
    reply_filter=vex_reply_filter
)

manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=groupchat,
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list}
)

# Production monitoring
monitor_id = vex_client.create_monitor(
    name="invoice_extraction",
    verifiers=["extraction_schema", "field_accuracy", "completeness"]
)

user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message=document_prompt)
